[[Category:grammatical numbers]]
The '''singular''' is the base, unmarked grammatical number in [[Aelaki]]. It represents a single instance of a noun and uses the unmodified root template.

== Formation ==

The singular is formed using the base C₁V₁C₂V₂C₃ template with no morphological operations applied. Because it is the default form, singular nouns require no affixation, [[Collective|umlaut]], or [[Aelaki plural|reduplication]].

{| class="wikitable"
! Root !! Singular Form !! Meaning
|-
| B-S-L || Basol || tree
|-
| SH-B-V || Shabav || man
|}

== Relationship to Other Numbers ==

The singular serves as the base from which all other grammatical numbers are derived through the four core morphological operations:

{| class="wikitable"
! Number !! Operation !! Template
|-
| '''Singular''' || Base (unmarked) || C₁V₁C₂V₂C₃
|-
| [[Aelaki plural|Plural]] || Reduplication of C₂ || C₁V₁C₂C₂V₂C₃
|-
| [[Collective]] || Umlaut (vowel fronting) || C₁frontV₁C₂frontV₂C₃
|-
| [[Zero]] || Zero-infix /f/ || C₁V₁fC₂V₂C₃
|}

== Ki Syllables ==

The [[Ki]] syllables encode number distinctions for each person-gender combination. The singular form of the Ki syllable is the base entry in each person-gender paradigm.

== See Also ==

* [[Aelaki plural|Plural]]
* [[Paucal]]
* [[Plentiful]]
* [[Collective]]
* [[Zero]]

[[Category:git synced pages]]
